title: Zero-Click Attacks: AI Agents as Weapons
modules: What Zero-Click Means and Why AI Agents Raise the Stakes
Attack Vectors: Email Parsing, Calendar Invites, and Agentic Pipelines
Case Studies: Real Zero-Click Exploits Amplified by Automation
How Prompt Injection Becomes a Zero-Click Trigger
Detection Strategies: Telemetry, Sandboxing, and Behavioral Baselines
Hardening Agentic Systems Against Unsolicited Input Exploitation
Red Team Lab: Simulating a Zero-Click Agent Compromise
synopsis: Zero-click attacks require no user action to execute — and as AI agents are granted access to email, files, and APIs, a single malicious input can silently chain through an entire organization's systems without anyone clicking anything. This course maps how attackers weaponize agentic architectures and equips security professionals to detect and contain these invisible intrusions.
rationale: The rapid enterprise adoption of AI agents with broad tool access has created a new class of zero-interaction attack surface that existing endpoint and perimeter defenses were never designed to cover.
learning_outcome: Learners can identify architectural conditions in agentic systems that enable zero-click exploitation, implement behavioral telemetry to detect anomalous agent actions, and apply hardening controls that limit blast radius when an agent is compromised.
source_signals: Zero-Click Attacks: AI Agents and the Next Cybersecurity Challenge
Comparing AI agents to cybersecurity professionals in real-world pen testing
